name: Centuryfirst
description_short: Federal credit union headquartered in Hattiesburg, MS with $140M in assets, 16,306 members, and 9 branches offering traditional banking services since 1956.
description_long:
CenturyFirst Federal Credit Union was established in 1956 and has operated for 70 years as a member-owned financial cooperative. Headquartered in Hattiesburg, Mississippi, the institution serves a membership base of approximately 16,306 people through 9 branch locations across the region. The credit union maintains total assets of $140.19 million and employs 52 full and part-time staff members.

CenturyFirst offers a comprehensive range of traditional banking and financial services designed for everyday consumers. Their product suite includes checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, real estate loans, unsecured personal loans, and various deposit products. The organization demonstrates specific commitment to financial accessibility through programs such as a Credit Builder offering, First Time Homebuyer Program, International Remittances, and Low Cost Wire Transfers. These services reflect an orientation toward serving members across different financial circumstances and life stages.

The credit union's membership structure emphasizes educational common bonds as a primary focus, distinguishing it as a community-focused institution rather than a for-profit bank. With a capital-to-assets ratio of approximately 14.4% and net income of $1.13 million in the most recent reporting period, CenturyFirst demonstrates financial stability. The organization's loan portfolio of $114.5 million is diversified across real estate ($42.2M), unsecured loans ($5.4M), and other loan categories ($67M), indicating balanced risk management.

CenturyFirst operates as a traditional, member-focused credit union without venture capital backing or technological disruption. While offering solid mainstream financial products, the institution does not position itself as a technology-forward neobank or provide specialized fintech services. Members should evaluate whether the credit union's community footprint and membership-based structure align with their banking preferences, particularly if they prioritize digital-first banking experiences or specialized financial products.
services: Savings accounts (Regular shares and deposit products)
Call Report and financial counseling services
pros: 70-year operating history since 1956 demonstrates institutional longevity and stability
Member-owned cooperative structure with educational focus aligns incentives with member benefits rather than shareholder profits
9 branch locations across Mississippi provide in-person banking access in underserved regions
Specific programs for underserved populations: Credit Builder, First Time Homebuyer Program, and International Remittances
Diversified loan portfolio ($114.5M) across real estate, unsecured, and other categories reduces concentration risk
Low Cost Wire Transfers and international remittance services indicate commitment to affordable financial access
Healthy capital position with 14.4% capital-to-assets ratio and $1.13M net income demonstrates financial strength
cons: Limited digital presence or information about online banking capabilities despite modern expectations
No FHLB membership may limit access to wholesale funding and liquidity resources available to larger institutions
9 branches confined to Mississippi limits geographical convenience for non-resident members or those relocating
No mention of investment products, wealth management, or premium banking services for affluent members
Relatively modest asset base of $140M limits scale for complex financial products or large loan accommodations
best_for: Mississippi residents seeking community-focused, member-owned financial institution with local branch presence
First-time homebuyers taking advantage of specific homebuyer programs and real estate lending expertise
Individuals building credit who benefit from Credit Builder loans and credit-focused product offerings
Members needing affordable international remittances or wire transfer services
diagnosis: CenturyFirst is best for Mississippi residents and educators seeking traditional credit union banking with community focus, membership benefits, and specific programs for homebuyers and credit building. The primary caveat is geographic limitation to Mississippi and lack of visible digital banking infrastructure—this is a conventional brick-and-mortar credit union without apparent technology differentiation or nationwide reach.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
